installing Julia on a cluster with the Spack package manager, there are a few problems with precompile.
Typically, the root user has performed an installation by issuing the command: `spack install julia@1.9.3%gcc@13.2.0`.
This creates a directory in: `/tmp/root/spack-stage` to compile Julia before installing it.
The installation finishes normally, but when a lambda cluster user calls Julia, there are problems with precompile.
Typically, you find an error like:

```bash
ERROR : LoadError : IOError : stat("/tmp/root/spack-stage/spack-stage-julia-1.10.2-2zigizyfch37c2tz5g5cu75wbnerf4zh/spack-src/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.10/Test/src/Test.jl ”) : permission denied (EACCES)
```

(test the SparseArrays package, for example, or even using it after adding it)

Now, if you delete the directory at its head, this seems to solve the problem, meaning that Julia tests for the presence of the installation directory, which normally results in a non-fatal file-not-found error, but in multi-user systems can result in permission denied errors instead.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55966/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55966/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2388704026","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55966#issuecomment-2388704026","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55966","id":2388704026,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6OYLsa","user":{"login":"giordano","id":765740,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc2NTc0MA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/765740?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano","html_url":"https://github.com/giordano","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-02T13:52:39Z","updated_at":"2024-10-02T13:52:39Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"To reproduce the issue outside of Spack with the official binaries:

installing Julia on a cluster with the Spack package manager, there are a few problems with precompile.
Typically, the root user has performed an installation by issuing the command: `spack install julia@1.9.3%gcc@13.2.0`.
This creates a directory in: `/tmp/root/spack-stage` to compile Julia before installing it.
The installation finishes normally, but when a lambda cluster user calls Julia, there are problems with precompile.
Typically, you find an error like:

```bash
ERROR : LoadError : IOError : stat("/tmp/root/spack-stage/spack-stage-julia-1.10.2-2zigizyfch37c2tz5g5cu75wbnerf4zh/spack-src/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.10/Test/src/Test.jl ”) : permission denied (EACCES)
```

(test the SparseArrays package, for example, or even using it after adding it)

Now, if you delete the directory at its head, this seems to solve the problem, meaning that Julia tests for the presence of the installation directory, which normally results in a non-fatal file-not-found error, but in multi-user systems can result in permission denied errors                file2 = joinpath(dir2, "file")
               touch(file2)
               samefile(file1, file2)
           end
       end

not sure what you mean by "same precision" as there's little correlation between the the integer datatype and the floating point datatype (the range of numbers they can represent is different and the distance between consecutive numbers is in general different), apart from having the same size for their canonical representations. Besides, the change you suggest would be breaking.

If you really want to do that and don't want to wait for a hypothetical Julia v2.0 (and no assurance this idea will be accepted anyway), surely you can define a custom function which achieves what you want.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2390998724/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

seems to fix #55966 for me on v1.10:
```julia-repl
julia> run(`sudo mkdir -p $(abspath(Sys.BUILD_STDLIB_PATH))`);

julia> run(`sudo chmod go-x $(abspath(Sys.BUILD_STDLIB_PATH))`);

julia> using SuiteSparse
[ Info: Precompiling SuiteSparse [4607b0f0-06f3-5cda-b6b1-a6196a1729e9]

julia>
```

It's hard to make a proper test for this because it requires fiddling with the build directory, which for from-source builds is likely needed and you don't want to mess with it, and in general may require admin access.

I'll make a separate PR for `master`, as sketched in https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55966#issuecomment-2389154120, I started from v1.10 because that's the only version where I can reliably reproduce the issue, and so test it locally easily.  Also, the code around these lines is changed in v1.11, so this would need a manual backport in any I don't think I did.

It was introduced in your PR to add pkgimages:
https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/47184/files#diff-ec80afe9f8ff182765c73ed3fc35a103b561f873a52b1dfe1ce135e4e5f9a2cfR2610 🙂  but commits were squashed, so hard to know who exactly wrote that line.

> So, is the issue here that "samefile" requires the path to exist on the filesystem?

It needs the paths to be `stat`-able, rather then existing: it's fine with non-existing files:
```
julia> samefile("/does/not/exist", "/neither/does/this")
false
```
but if the parent directories are unreadable, then `samefile` (or `stat`) throws out seems to fix #55966 for me on v1.10:
```julia-repl
julia> run(`sudo mkdir -p $(abspath(Sys.BUILD_STDLIB_PATH))`);

julia> run(`sudo chmod go-x $(abspath(Sys.BUILD_STDLIB_PATH))`);

julia> using SuiteSparse
[ Info: Precompiling SuiteSparse [4607b0f0-06f3-5cda-b6b1-a6196a1729e9]

julia>
```

It's hard to make a proper test for this because it requires fiddling with the build directory, which for from-source builds is likely needed and you don't want to mess with it, and in general may require admin access.

I'll make a separate PR for `master`, as sketched in https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55966#issuecomment-2389154120, I started from v1.10 because that's the only version where I can reliably reproduce the issue, and so test it locally easily.  Also, the code around these lines is changed in v1.11, so this would need a manual backport in any seems to fix #55966 for me on v1.10:
```julia-repl
julia> run(`sudo mkdir -p $(abspath(Sys.BUILD_STDLIB_PATH))`);

julia> run(`sudo chmod go-x $(abspath(Sys.BUILD_STDLIB_PATH))`);

julia> using SuiteSparse
[ Info: Precompiling SuiteSparse [4607b0f0-06f3-5cda-b6b1-a6196a1729e9]

julia>
```

It's hard to make a proper test for this because it requires fiddling with the build directory, which for from-source builds is likely needed and you don't want to mess with it, and in general may require admin access.

I'll make a separate PR for `master`, as sketched in https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55966#issuecomment-2389154120, I started from v1.10 because that's the only version where I can reliably reproduce the issue, and so test it locally easily.  Also, the code around these lines is changed in v1.11, so this would need a manual backport in any
